reason: Medicare Advantage represents a $300B+ annual market with deep regulatory capture mechanics. Title signals potential carve-outs, payment floor protections, or reduced Star Rating penalties—classic vectors for managed care operator ROI at taxpayer cost.
vectors: regulatory_capture: MA insurers seeking exemptions from quality/utilization penalties
fiscal_bleed: payment rate floors or risk-adjustment manipulation that expand MA costs vs. traditional Medicare
market_access_exploit: reduced enrollment/disenrollment friction benefiting incumbent plans
entitlement_erosion: potential benefit narrowing or cost-shift to beneficiaries disguised as 'improvement'
title: Medicare Advantage Improvement Act of 2026

reason: RAC (Recovery Audit Contractor) bills historically create regulatory capture vectors: private audit firms gain asymmetric enforcement power over provider networks while insulating themselves from liability. Without summary text, high uncertainty, but the framing suggests potential safe-harbor carve-outs for RAC operations and/or provider payment restructuring that favors positioned auditors.
vectors: regulatory_capture: private RAC contractors gain expanded audit authority with potential liability shields
entitlement_erosion: RAC expansion historically accelerates Medicaid payment denials and provider network collapse
fiscal_misdirect: RAC 'savings' claims often obscure true administrative costs and access damage
title: Medicaid RAC Improvement Act of 2026
